steps.title: Recognize the difference of squares
steps.content: Inside the parentheses, $x^2-100y^2$ is a difference of squares because $100y^2=(10y)^2$. Use the pattern $a^2-b^2=(a-b)(a+b)$.

steps.title: Check the choice in Desmos
steps.content:
Use two test points to catch the wrong-factor choices:
$$x_1=[11,12]$$
$$y_1=[1,2]$$
$$o_1=17(x_1^2-100y_1^2)$$
$$d_1=17(x_1-10y_1)(x_1+10y_1)$$
The original and choice D produce the same output list.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: $17(x-10y)(x+10y)$ is choice D, so D is correct.
